description: Near-miss probe (issue #183, docs/evaluation.md D1 near-miss set): counterparty reformats Section 8 but KEEPS the 'consequential damages' waiver verbatim (and, as a sibling, keeps '$150,000'/'aggregate liability' intact too, so this fixture does not accidentally trip 'preserve-liability-cap'). Must ACCEPT with zero fires on 'preserve-consequential-damages-waiver'.

must_not_flag.reason: A cosmetic reformat that verbatim-preserves 'consequential damages' must not fire the token-removal Floor rule.

planted_variation.altered_hunk: Section 8 is reformatted for readability: neither party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of this Agreement, and each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed $150,000.
planted_variation.notes: Near-miss: cosmetic reformat; retains required_token 'consequential damages' and the sibling tokens '$150,000'/'aggregate liability'.

detector_expectation.rationale: Altered hunk retains required_token 'consequential damages'; token_policy='any' does not fire when nothing required is missing.

authorship_note: AFK-drafted per issue #183 (owner-approved synthetic scaffold, 2026-07-10): fully invented, no real-world contract text, no de-identification required. Marked synthetic and pending GC sign-off; not production-qualified until Legal/GC reviews and approves.
